# @overflow-aware-list/svelte

> Svelte 5 component that renders only the items that physically fit inside a container. Items that overflow are hidden and passed to a `renderMore` snippet so you can show a "+N" badge, ellipsis, or any custom indicator. Supports horizontal and vertical directions, and can slice from either end of the list.

- GitHub: https://github.com/rai-aashish/overflow-aware-list/tree/main/packages/svelte
- Live demo: https://rai-aashish.github.io/overflow-aware-list/svelte

## Installation

```bash
npm install @overflow-aware-list/svelte
```

Peer dependency: `svelte >= 5`.

## Quick start

```svelte
<script>
  import { OverflowList } from "@overflow-aware-list/svelte";
  const tags = ["React", "TypeScript", "Vite", "Tailwind", "Zustand"];
</script>

<OverflowList items={tags}>
  {#snippet renderItem(tag)}
    <span class="tag">{tag}</span>
  {/snippet}
  {#snippet renderMore(hidden)}
    <span class="badge">+{hidden.length}</span>
  {/snippet}
</OverflowList>
```

## Props

```ts
type OverflowListProps<T> = {
  /** Array of items to display. */
  items: T[];

  /** Layout axis. Default: "horizontal". */
  direction?: "horizontal" | "vertical";

  /**
   * Which end to keep visible when overflow occurs.
   * "start" — show first N items, overflow indicator at end (default).
   * "end"   — show last N items, overflow indicator at start (useful for breadcrumbs).
   */
  sliceFrom?: "start" | "end";

  /** Snippet to render a single visible item. Receives (item, originalIndex). */
  renderItem: Snippet<[T, number]>;

  /**
   * Snippet to render the overflow indicator. Receives the array of hidden items.
   * Also rendered (with an empty array) in the off-screen layer so its size is accounted for.
   */
  renderMore: Snippet<[T[]]>;

  /**
   * HTML tag for the outer container element. Default: "div".
   * Generic — rest props and `ref` are typed to match the chosen tag.
   * @example renderAs="nav"  // ref becomes HTMLElement, restProps accepts nav attrs
   * @example renderAs="button"  // ref becomes HTMLButtonElement, restProps accepts button attrs
   */
  renderAs?: Tag; // Tag extends keyof HTMLElementTagNameMap

  /** HTML tag for each item wrapper element. Default: "div". */
  itemTag?: string;

  /** CSS class forwarded to the container element. */
  class?: string;

  /** Bindable ref to the container element. */
  ref?: HTMLElementTagNameMap[Tag] | null;
} & Omit<HTMLAttributes<HTMLElementTagNameMap[Tag]>, "class">;
// Tag defaults to "div". Rest props and ref narrow automatically when renderAs is set.
// All other HTML attributes are spread onto the container element.
```

## Usage examples

### Horizontal tag row with "+N" badge

```svelte
<script>
  import { OverflowList } from "@overflow-aware-list/svelte";
  const tags = ["React", "TypeScript", "Vite", "Tailwind", "Zustand"];
</script>

<div style="width: 300px">
  <OverflowList items={tags}>
    {#snippet renderItem(tag)}
      <span class="tag">{tag}</span>
    {/snippet}
    {#snippet renderMore(hidden)}
      <span class="badge">+{hidden.length}</span>
    {/snippet}
  </OverflowList>
</div>
```

### Breadcrumb — show deepest path segments

```svelte
<OverflowList
  items={crumbs}
  direction="horizontal"
  sliceFrom="end"
>
  {#snippet renderItem(crumb, i)}
    <span class="crumb">
      {#if i > 0}<span>/</span>{/if}
      {crumb}
    </span>
  {/snippet}
  {#snippet renderMore(hidden)}
    <span>/…{hidden.length}/</span>
  {/snippet}
</OverflowList>
```

### Vertical notification feed

```svelte
<div style="height: 160px">
  <OverflowList items={notifications} direction="vertical">
    {#snippet renderItem(n)}
      <div class="notif">{n.text}</div>
    {/snippet}
    {#snippet renderMore(hidden)}
      <div>+{hidden.length} more</div>
    {/snippet}
  </OverflowList>
</div>
```

### Custom container tag (e.g. `<nav>`, `<ul>`)

```svelte
<OverflowList items={crumbs} renderAs="nav" itemTag="span">
  {#snippet renderItem(crumb)}...{/snippet}
  {#snippet renderMore(hidden)}...{/snippet}
</OverflowList>
```

### Bindable ref to the container

```svelte
<script>
  let containerEl = $state(null);
</script>

<OverflowList items={...} bind:ref={containerEl}>
  ...
</OverflowList>
```

## Styling

The component renders a `<{renderAs} data-slot="overflow-list">` as the container. It forces these styles via `style:` directives (you cannot override them via `style` prop):

```
display: flex
align-items: start
justify-content: start
overflow: hidden
flex-direction: row | column   (from direction prop)
width: 100%                    (horizontal only)
height: 100%                   (vertical only)
```

Any attributes/props you pass (e.g. `class`, `style`, `data-*`) are spread onto the container first; layout-critical `style:` directives always win. Set `gap` via `class` or `style` — the component reads it from computed styles to account for it in calculations.

Each item is wrapped in `<{itemTag} data-overflow-item style:flex-shrink="0">`.

## How it works

1. Two render passes: visible container (clipped with `overflow: hidden`) + off-screen measurement layer (`position: absolute; visibility: hidden; left: -9999px`).
2. `ResizeObserver` watches the container, each item, and the "more" indicator.
3. On any size change, the controller computes how many items fit and updates reactive state.
4. Svelte re-renders with the new `visibleItems` / `hiddenItems` split.

## Implementation notes (Svelte 5 specifics)

- Uses `$props()`, `$state()`, `$effect()`, and `$bindable()` runes — requires Svelte 5.
- Controller is constructed inside `untrack()` so it captures only the initial prop values; a separate `$effect` keeps it in sync via `updateOptions()`.
- `bind:this` on the off-screen item wrappers feeds into `itemEls` array; a `$effect` registers them with the controller.
- SSR renders all items; client trims after first `ResizeObserver` callback.
